channel.title: Tags on CEDIS
channel.description: Recent content in Tags on CEDIS

item.title: Software Engineering students' thesis gives rise to Git Ranking, a gamified application for agile teams on GitHub
item.description: A bachelor's thesis supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas resulted in Git Ranking, a gamified application integrated with GitHub.

item.title: DevDog: undergraduate thesis turns code smells into gamified learning
item.description: Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis that gave rise to DevDog, a gamified web application for teaching code smell identification.

item.title: DevOps under the DORA lens: a portrait from the financial sector
item.description: A dissertation defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program proposes a framework to assess DevOps maturity in banking teams by combining DORA metrics, document analysis and sociotechnical triangulation.

item.title: FTV Scout: an undergraduate thesis brings performance analysis to footvolley
item.description: Felipe Direito Corrieri de Macedo defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing FTV Scout — a system for notation and performance analysis of footvolley athletes and teams.

item.title: Counting function points without humans counting: a dissertation reads HTTP requests to automate SFP
item.description: Diego Emanuel Ferreira da Rocha presented at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program the qualification exam of a dissertation that automates elementary-process identification through HTTP request analysis — reducing the friction of Simple Function Points.

item.title: 3SW: master's dissertation proposes adapting CIS Controls v8.1 to Brazilian public administration
item.description: Tássio Correia da Silva defended at UnB’s Electrical Engineering graduate program a professional master’s dissertation adapting the CIS Controls v8.1 model to mitigate threats to Web servers in the Brazilian public administration.

item.title: Three Filters: an undergraduate thesis filters scientific metadata in three layers
item.description: Leonardo Milomes Vitoriano and Mateus de Almeida Dias defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis presenting Three Filters — a system to ensure quality and integrity of scientific metadata in JSON.

item.title: A chatbot for FCTE: an undergraduate thesis centralizes administrative information for students
item.description: Breno Henrique de Souza and Giulia Domingues de Alcantara defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis centralizing FCTE administrative information into an AI-supported chatbot.

item.title: Gamifying Scrum: an undergraduate thesis maps the literature and drafts guidelines for the field
item.description: Denniel Roriz Lima defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis running a bibliometric and thematic review on the use of gamification in agile processes with Scrum, and proposing practical guidelines for implementation.

item.title: Algorithmic bias: an undergraduate thesis systematically maps the ethical challenges
item.description: Amanda Jeniffer Pereira Nobre and Ana Carolina Rodrigues Leite defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis running a systematic literature mapping on the ethical challenges tied to bias in AI algorithms.

item.title: Mental health app at Campus Gama: an undergraduate thesis delivers a tool designed with students in mind
item.description: Delziron Braz de Lima and Fellipe Pereira da Costa Silva defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing an app to support mental health and well-being of students at Campus Gama.

item.title: Predicting euploidy without touching the embryo: an undergraduate thesis brings machine learning to IVF
item.description: Maria Eduarda dos Santos Abritta Ferreira and Sabrina Caldas Berno defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying machine learning to the non-invasive prediction of euploidy in human embryos, based on morphokinetic data.

item.title: TROPA: an undergraduate thesis designs software to orchestrate airsoft operations
item.description: Erick Giffoni Felicissimo defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis designing and developing TROPA — Airsoft Theater of Operations — a software to support match organization in Brazil.

item.title: SmartPayment: an undergraduate thesis brings smart contracts to developer evaluation
item.description: João Pedro Elias de Moura defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis designing SmartPayment — a smart-contract-based system to automate assessment and payment in software development.

item.title: AgroMart migrates from IaaS to serverless: student duo shows small farmers can stay inside the free tier
item.description: Kalebe Cunha and Murilo Santana defended in February 2025 at UnB’s Engineering Faculty an undergraduate thesis that migrates AgroMart from IaaS to AWS Lambda and tests the feasibility of running the app within the free tier — dropping cost for farmers.

item.title: Chat gpt 4o in requirements elicitation an undergraduate thesis runs the case study the community needed
item.description: Geraldo Victor Alves Barbosa and João Victor de Oliveira Matos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating the use of ChatGPT-4o in the requirements elicitation, discovery, and specification process.

item.title: gov.br security: a dissertation evaluates additional authentication factors for Brazil's digital identity
item.description: Sandro Leite Furtado defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program a dissertation identifying and evaluating additional security factors for the gov.br identity, implementing and testing Risk-Based Authentication (RBA) as a complementary layer.

item.title: OMA: a duo builds an academic multimedia organizer for engineering students
item.description: Artur de Sousa Vieira and Luíza Esteves dos Santos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing OMA — Academic Multimedia Organizer — aimed at Campus Gama engineering students.

item.title: SiCaPEx: a dissertation audits usability in Brazilian Army's personnel system
item.description: Dione Ramos Canuto Moura defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program a dissertation analyzing the usability of SiCaPEx — the Brazilian Army personnel registration system — combining SUS, Nielsen’s heuristics, and implemented improvement proposals.

item.title: Rooms, timeslots, lecturers: a dissertation proposes an adaptive model to schedule academic resources at UnB
item.description: Éber Junio Borges Moreira defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation tackling academic resource scheduling in higher education institutions, with a case study on UnB itself.

item.title: Labor law in your pocket: a PWA brings legal flowcharts to mobile devices
item.description: João Pedro Alves Machado and Júlio César Martins França defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis building a Progressive Web App to navigate legal-education flowcharts on labor rights.

item.title: Forms that teach law: an undergraduate thesis models legal flows in Google Forms
item.description: Guilherme Rogelin Vial and Lucas Braun Vieira Xavier defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis using Google Forms as the interface to model and distribute navigation flows for legal education.

item.title: AgroMart reaches publication: a duo integrates and connects the app to end users
item.description: Christian Fleury Alencar Siqueira and Thiago Siqueira Gomes defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis crossing AgroMart’s last mile: integrating modules, tuning deployment, and publishing the app for public use.

item.title: Accessibility tools and an ageing Brazil: an undergraduate thesis compares what the market offers
item.description: Daniel Barcelos Moreira and Juliana Pereira Valle Gonçalves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis evaluating tools used to measure the accessibility of web sites used by elderly people.

item.title: AI in evaluating software courses: a pilot undergraduate thesis tests support for teachers
item.description: Caio César Oliveira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing artificial intelligence to improve individual evaluations in Software Engineering courses.

item.title: AI model for Libras: an undergraduate thesis crosses the communication barrier with the deaf community
item.description: Daniela Soares de Oliveira and Danilo Domingo Vitoriano Silva defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis building an AI model for Brazilian Sign Language translation — a technical response to a communication barrier affecting more than 2.3 million Brazilians.

item.title: Llms for legal labor consultation an undergraduate thesis presents a multimodal chatbot
item.description: Álvaro Henrique de Sousa Gouvea and Luís Guilherme Gaboardi Lins defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis using Large Language Models to build a multimodal chatbot for legal-labor consultation.

item.title: Gamifying Moodle without becoming a programmer: an undergraduate thesis delivers a guide and tool for instructors
item.description: Antonio Rangel Chaves and Vitor Diniz Pagani Vieira Ribeiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis lowering the entry barrier for teachers who want to gamify Moodle courses — with a guide and a practical tool.

item.title: GCMP: a dissertation systematizes how to build gamified chatbots
item.description: Arthur Rocha Temporim de Lacerda defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program a dissertation proposing GCMP — the Gamified Chatbot Management Process — a method to orchestrate the end-to-end development of gamified chatbots.

item.title: Continuous improvement focused on usability: an undergraduate thesis applies established mechanisms in a web app
item.description: Francisco Heronildo Sousa Santos and João Vitor Ferreira Alves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying established usability mechanisms in an evaluation-and-improvement cycle on a web application.

item.title: CSA association in the cloud: AgroMart gains a piece to articulate communities
item.description: Abner Filipe Cunha Ribeiro and Rafael Leão Teixeira de Magalhães defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis expanding AgroMart to link CSA associations in the cloud — an important step in consolidating the platform.

item.title: Accessibility metrics for visually impaired users: an undergraduate thesis distills audit practice
item.description: Ricardo de Castro Loureiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing metrics to evaluate the accessibility of web pages for people with visual impairment — a direct step toward more objective audit practices.

item.title: Gamifying a public enterprise: dissertation studies how and why the strategy (sometimes) engages
item.description: Luciana Santos de Assis defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program a dissertation critically analyzing gamification implementation in organizational contexts — through a case study inside a Brazilian public enterprise.

item.title: Improving the requirements process: an undergraduate thesis brings rigor to development's most volatile cycle
item.description: Giulia Lobo Barros defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating improvement of the software requirements engineering process — under joint advising of Cristiane Ramos and Ricardo Ajax.

item.title: Latte: a duo trains a gamified chatbot to help write scientific articles
item.description: Luiz Henrique Fernandes Zamprogno and Victor Eduardo Araújo Ribeiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing Latte — a gamified chatbot to support the production of scientific articles.

item.title: Distance learning in Software Engineering: a thesis listens to students and measures their real perception
item.description: Júlia Farias Sousa defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis analyzing student perceptions on the use of distance learning in the FCTE/UnB Software Engineering course.

item.title: From guidance to conversation: a thesis studies ChatGPT as a complement in software engineering
item.description: Rafael Berto Pereira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating the complementary use of ChatGPT in software engineering processes — through a case study with previously defined guidelines.

item.title: BI for mental health: an undergraduate thesis builds dashboards for the Psychosocial Care Network
item.description: Luis Bruno Fidelis Gomes defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing Business Intelligence dashboards for monitoring by professionals of Brazil’s Psychosocial Care Network.

item.title: AgroMart payment module: a duo removes the friction that separated producers and consumers
item.description: Felipe Boccardi Silva Agustini and Giovanna Borges Bottino defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis integrating a payment module into AgroMart and, in the process, taking on the evolutionary maintenance of the platform.

item.title: How much will that Lambda cost? An undergraduate thesis predicts execution time in serverless
item.description: Lucas Ganda Carvalho and Wíctor Bastos Girardi defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating execution-time prediction for AWS Lambda functions — a key input for cost estimation in serverless environments.

item.title: LGPD inside the app: an undergraduate thesis turns privacy requirements into code
item.description: Lucas Lopes Xavier defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis translating LGPD and ISO 29100 requirements into implementable specifications for applications — shortening the distance between legal norms and engineering practice.

item.title: App supports gamified HCI course: a thesis delivers a tool built for the real classroom
item.description: Ítalo Alves Guimarães defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis implementing an application to support the gamified Human-Computer Interaction course — a piece the very use in class had been asking for.

item.title: Jornada role playing games enter the classroom as a learning process
item.description: João Vitor Lopes de Farias defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis examining the use of RPGs as the backbone of a learning process — combining narrative, roles and challenges with clear pedagogical goals.

item.title: Dropout at UnB under the machine learning lens: a dissertation predicts who is at risk
item.description: Edmilson Cosme da Silva defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation applying machine learning to predict academic dropout in UnB’s on-campus undergraduate programs.

item.title: Software quality and an ageing Brazil: an undergraduate thesis reads TIC Household data
item.description: Guilherme Daniel Fernandes da Silva and Lorrayne Alves Cardozo defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis analyzing the relationship between software quality and population ageing using data from Brazil’s TIC Household survey.

item.title: Police intelligence reports under the gamification lens: a dissertation measures what changes
item.description: Públio Pastrolin Cavalcante defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation evaluating how gamification applied to police intelligence activities affects the quality of reports produced.

item.title: Why don't companies use processes they already paid to define?
item.description: Nathalia Lorena Cardoso Dias defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis using a systematic literature review to investigate why formally defined software improvement processes are frequently unused in practice.

item.title: SAMI: an undergraduate thesis creates a system to analyze learning metrics
item.description: João Vitor de Moura Rosa Silva defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis building SAMI — a system for analyzing learning indicator metrics in active-methodology courses.

item.title: Tailored deploy: an undergraduate thesis automates individualized AgroMart deployments
item.description: André Aben-Athar de Freitas and Pedro Vitor de Salles Cella defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis automating the individualized deployment process of AgroMart — allowing each CSA to run its own instance.

item.title: FormsGeo: a thesis adds geolocation to social surveys and returns context to respondents
item.description: Marcelo Victor de Sousa Silva and Washington Bispo Arishita Júnior defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing FormsGeo — an app for social surveys with built-in geolocation.

item.title: Ia qvt an accessible tool to measure quality of work life
item.description: Gabriel de Jesus Carvalho defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing IA-QVT — a tool to collect and process data on quality of work life, with strong attention to accessibility.

item.title: $tudy: an undergraduate thesis brings financial education into a gamified system
item.description: Rafael Santos Teodosio defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis designing and implementing $tudy — a gamified system aimed at supporting the financial education of young people.

item.title: E gov and the queue for the elderly an undergraduate thesis studies accessibility barriers in digital public services
item.description: Fabiana Luiza Vasconcelos Pfeilsticker Ribas defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis analyzing the accessibility of Brazilian digital public services for elderly people — an agenda that gained urgency with the accelerated digitalization during the pandemic.

item.title: Factors that push students out: a duo studies dropout in higher education
item.description: Amanda Emilly Muniz de Menezes and Letícia Karla Soares Rodrigues de Araújo defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis identifying academic, demographic and learning factors associated with higher education dropout — and testing predictive models.

item.title: QVT in the public sector: an undergraduate thesis proposes a web tool to monitor quality of work life
item.description: João Gabriel Rossi and Paulo Vitor Coelho da Rocha defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing a web tool to support quality of work life promotion in public sector agencies.

item.title: Serious games for ADHD: an undergraduate thesis surfaces requirements the literature usually skips
item.description: Gabriel Batista Albino Silva and Helena Bretas Goulart defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis identifying non-functional requirements for serious games aimed at children with Attention-Deficit / Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD).

item.title: V&V in the public sector: an undergraduate thesis documents process improvement in a state software factory
item.description: Renan Cristyan Araujo Pinheiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis presenting a case study on improving software verification and validation processes in a public sector agency.

item.title: University of Heuristics: an undergraduate thesis turns HCI teaching into a serious game
item.description: Cauê Mateus Oliveira and Lucas Midlhey Cardoso Naves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis turning the study of Nielsen’s heuristics into a game — the University of Heuristics.

item.title: Agro mart evolves an undergraduate thesis opens the source, adds payments, and manages co farmers
item.description: Byron Kamal Barreto Correa and Igor Guimaraes Veludo defended at FCTE/UnB an important step in AgroMart’s story: open-sourcing the codebase, integrating payment methods, and designing the co-farmer management module.

item.title: Pixel: an undergraduate thesis gamifies learning of usability criteria
item.description: Érico Maximiano Bandeira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing Pixel — a gamified tool for learning usability criteria during interface evaluation.

item.title: Early stage startups and software maintenance an undergraduate thesis documents the state of practice
item.description: Filipe Toyoshima Silva defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis mapping how early-stage startups handle software maintenance — a phase dominated by scarce resources and high change volume.

item.title: What users say when no one asks: an undergraduate thesis mines Play Store reviews for usability signals
item.description: Augusto Moreno Vilarins Cardoso da Silva and Ícaro Pereira de Oliveira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis designing a semi-automated process to extract usability signals from user comments on the Google Play Store.

item.title: Which framework to use when gamifying a class? A systematic review draws the map
item.description: Weiller Fernandes Pereira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis carrying out a systematic literature review on the use of frameworks in building education-oriented gamification projects.

item.title: Volunteer team, asynchronous testing: an undergraduate thesis builds a QA strategy in pandemic times
item.description: Vítor Cardoso Xoteslem defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis crafting a testing strategy for the Doarti app — maintained by a geographically distributed volunteer team under pandemic-forced remote work.

item.title: REQGAME: a dissertation tests gamification for requirements elicitation in a federal public agency
item.description: Ana Cláudia Alves Mendes Araújo defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation applying gamification to the requirements elicitation process in software projects run at a federal public agency.

item.title: Analytics inside the classroom: an undergraduate thesis uses data mining to decide where to tune TBL
item.description: Allan Jefrey Pereira Nobre defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying Learning Analytics and Educational Data Mining to support pedagogical decisions in Team-Based Learning courses.

item.title: AgroMart is born: a duo introduces a platform for family farming in a 2021 undergraduate thesis
item.description: Lucas Pereira de Andrade Macêdo and Lucas Siqueira Rodrigues defended at FCTE/UnB the undergraduate thesis that gives birth to AgroMart — a platform to bring small farmers closer to consumers of organic and artisanal products.

item.title: A junior enterprise, a live experiment: an undergraduate thesis evaluates gamification applied on the ground
item.description: Ateldy Borges Brasil Filho defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis evaluating gamification applied in a junior enterprise — an organization with rotating teams, short cycles, and high initial motivation.

item.title: Interfaces that induce error: an undergraduate thesis catalogues common traps — and how to avoid them
item.description: Caio César de Almeida Beleza defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis analyzing software interface problems that push users toward error and systematizing recommendations to avoid them.

item.title: Reading what's already logged: an undergraduate thesis proposes planning process improvements from issues
item.description: Sannya Santana de Arvelos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing the use of issue analysis as the basis for identifying software process improvement opportunities.

item.title: Software process improvement: where to start when everything seems chaotic?
item.description: Rafael Contessotto Bragança Pinheiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing an approach to help organizations kick off software process improvement programs — the hardest step when almost everything demands attention.

item.title: Exploratory testing in action: an undergraduate thesis pairs scripted and free approaches to evaluate software
item.description: Iolane Caroline Alves de Andrade defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying exploratory testing alongside traditional testing, highlighting the learning gain that exploratory testing brings to the process.

item.title: Refactoring as leverage: an undergraduate thesis shows how to rewrite code for variability
item.description: Ronyell Henrique dos Santos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying refactoring operations to improve software system variability — reducing maintenance costs over the lifecycle.

item.title: UnB's Service Bus gains monitoring: a dissertation instruments the university's digital backbone
item.description: Felipe Evangelista dos Santos defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation integrating monitoring tools into UnB’s Service Bus — critical infrastructure for communication between academic systems.

item.title: SOA, agile, and QA in the same case study: a dissertation documents where three cultures actually meet
item.description: James Taylor Faria Chaves defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation analyzing, through a case study, the encounter between Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), agile development methods, and Quality Assurance practices.

item.title: Team based learning gets a platform undergraduate thesis moves the methodology from the classroom to the cloud
item.description: Victor Hugo Arnaud Deon defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing PGTBL — an open-source platform to manage Team-Based Learning activities in university courses.

item.title: Who wrote that commit? An undergraduate thesis computes authorship rates inside Git projects
item.description: Laércio Silva de Sousa Júnior defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating ways to compute developer authorship rates inside Git-versioned projects.

item.title: Measure to engage: an undergraduate thesis proposes a strategy to improve gamified applications
item.description: Paulo Markes Rodrigues de Oliveira Calado defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing a strategy to identify improvement opportunities in gamified applications — tackling the problem that the initial shine does not always translate into prolonged use.

item.title: Drivers as sensors: an undergraduate thesis proposes a crowdsourced app to monitor city fleets
item.description: Jonathan Paiva and Luis Henrique Guimarães defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis replacing sensors and cameras with human collaboration to monitor cars across a city — the NotifiCar app.

item.title: Making the invisible visible: an undergraduate thesis proposes a method to expose technical debt in agile teams
item.description: Iago Rodrigues Gonçalves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating how to identify, measure and communicate technical debt — a problem that gains weight in agile methodologies focused on continuous delivery.

item.title: Attracting is easy, retaining is gamification: an undergraduate thesis fights churn in an academic social network
item.description: Tiago Ribeiro de Assunção defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying gamification to the About Social Network to fight its weak spot: the network attracts new users easily but fails to retain them.

item.title: Math, children, and digital victories: the software that wants to turn a class into a mission
item.description: Leandro Alves Ferreira Santos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing gamified software to support math teaching for children — a territory poorly served by edtech products.

item.title: Experience bases: what to do with the knowledge that walks in and out of R&D labs
item.description: Attany Nathaly Lima Araújo defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis designing a process for building experience bases in R&D labs — a classic knowledge management challenge applied to software engineering.

item.title: Ciência sem Fronteiras through an engineering lens: what students brought back besides English
item.description: Leandro Veloso Rodrigues defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating the impact of Brazil’s Ciência sem Fronteiras program on the soft skills of future software engineers.

item.title: A tool to help teams build gamification projects without starting from scratch
item.description: Ludimila da Bela Cruz defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing a tool to support teams building gamification projects, anchoring the process in structured motivational elements.

item.title: Before the generative AI boom, an undergraduate thesis was already teaching machines to grade essays
item.description: Back in 2016, well before the LLM frenzy, Cristóvão Frinhani defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis that uses semantic similarity and machine learning to support distance-learning tutors grading essay questions.

item.title: Algorithms with points: an undergraduate thesis adapts a virtual environment to gamified programming teaching
item.description: Wilker Mesquita de Assunção defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis adapting a virtual learning environment to teach algorithms using gamification techniques.

item.title: Choosing a CMS without falling back on 'that's what we already use'
item.description: Thiago Silveira Honorato defended an undergraduate thesis proposing a rational method to choose between Content Management Systems for different scenarios — reducing reliance on decisions based on team familiarity alone.

item.title: Milliseconds matter: an undergraduate thesis measures the impact of code choices on Android
item.description: Victor Hugo Alves de Carvalho defended in 2015 at FCTE/UnB an empirical undergraduate thesis comparing programming techniques on Android and measuring the effect of each on app performance and responsiveness.

item.title: Ontologies to help mapmakers: an undergraduate thesis tackles the hardest step of systematic mapping
item.description: Igor de Araújo Ramos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing the use of ontologies to support the thorniest step of systematic mappings: turning collected data into a knowledge map that is actually usable.
